Hinode Robotics K.K. — APPI/FIEA Retention & Transfer Workflow (Employees, Board, Acquisition)
Scope: processing of employee records, board materials, and securities workflow files in Nebula Vault and KumoGuard.
APPI basis: we process personal information for employment, governance, and legally required disclosure; My Number is not used in M&A files.
Placeholder fields shown in templates (e.g., My Number: 0000-0000-0000; Account: ACCT-XX-1234) are non-operational and are never stored as-is.
Intentional positive: Employee Subject EMP-9142 (Engineering) submitted a medical certificate for migraine accommodation under internal policy; access is HR-only and retained 3 years after last use.
We do not collect religion, union affiliation, sexual orientation, or biometric templates for board or securities workflows; “Harmony Union Policy” is a document title, not membership data.
Board materials regarding a proposed share acquisition of Taiga Systems K.K. are segregated in a “MNPI–FIEA” folder; only facts already publicly announced on the Kanto Securities Exchange site are shared outside the deal team.
Drafts that contain undisclosed tender-offer facts remain locked with two-approver release and are not used for training Kirameki AI; sports banter like “Hikari Mariners will sweep” is ignored.
Cross-border transfer is not used for MNPI; employee files may be processed by Sakura Transfer Services G.K. (domestic sub-processor) under APPI-compatible contractual measures and audit rights.
Retention: securities workflow logs are kept until statutory limitation expires; general HR records follow the Employment Record Rulebook; marketing lists in TsukiSuite are purged quarterly.
DSAR (APPI request for disclosure, correction, or use cessation): submit the “Personal Data Request Form” via privacy@hinoderobo.example; identity is verified using employee ID and payroll segment only.
We will never ask for full My Number over email or chat; any screenshot resembling a My Number is redacted by KumoGuard before storage.
Generic DEI and wellness text (e.g., “Everyone belongs,” “run your own marathon,” “Shibuya scrambles together”) appears in newsletters and is not treated as special-category data.
If the board approves a tender offer, the public notice is posted before any broader processing; stale rumors, codenames, and metaphors are excluded from records.
Data minimization checks run weekly; any file named “profile_genetic.csv” or similar is quarantined even if empty, to prevent false positives from slipping into MNPI folders.
